sections.paragraph:
ταλαιπωρέω (ταλαίπωρος) impf. ἐταλαιπώρουν; fut. 3 pl. ταλαιπωρήσουσιν Hos 10:2; 1 aor. ἐταλαιπώρησα; pf. 3 sg. τεταλαιπώρηκεν LXX; occasionally act. ‘torment, afflict’ (Isocr. 8, 19; Ps 16:9; Is 33:1); pass. in act./intr. sense (Thu. 3, 3, 1 al.; Philo, In Flacc. 155; Jos., Ant. 2, 334; 5, 147 al.) to experience distress, endure sorrow/distress, be miserable (Eur., Thu. et al.; Epict. 1, 26, 11; LXX; Manetho in Jos., C. Ap. 1, 237; SibOr 5, 75) 2 Cl 19:4; Hv 3, 7, 1; Hs 6, 2, 7; 6, 3, 1. To the extent of giving expression to grief: the act. used intr. ταλαιπωρήσατε feel miserable, lament (w. πενθεῖν, κλαίειν) Js 4:9.—Spicq.

impf. impf. = imperfect

fut. fut. = fut.

  1. pl. = plural

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pf. = perfect
  2. sg. = singular

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

act. act. = active

Isocr Isocr , IV b.c.—List 5

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

intr. intr. = intransitive

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Manetho Manetho , hist., III b.c.—List 5

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

2 Cl 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

Hv Hv = Visions

Hs Hs = Similitudes

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 988
